description: NVIDIA Corporation designs graphics processing units (GPUs) and system-on-chip units. It dominates the AI accelerator market with its H100 and Blackwell GPU architectures.
known_facts: NVIDIA's H100 and A100 GPUs are the dominant AI training accelerators
Data Center segment surpassed Gaming as NVIDIA's largest revenue source
Jensen Huang is NVIDIA's founder and CEO
CUDA software platform creates high switching costs for AI developers
NVIDIA's revenue grew over 100% year-over-year in 2024
Blackwell GPU architecture launched in 2024 as successor to Hopper
NVIDIA supplies chips to Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta, and major cloud providers
NVIDIA holds over 80% market share in AI training accelerators
known_risks: US export restrictions limiting sales of advanced chips to China
Customer concentration risk — top hyperscaler customers account for large revenue share
AMD and Intel developing competing AI accelerator chips
Potential bubble risk if AI capex spending decelerates
Supply chain constraints limiting ability to meet surging demand
Geopolitical risk given chip manufacturing in Taiwan (TSMC)
known_strengths: Near-monopoly position in AI GPU market with 80%+ share
CUDA ecosystem creates massive developer lock-in
First-mover advantage in AI infrastructure hardware
Gross margins exceeding 70% on data center products
Continuous chip architecture innovation maintaining performance lead
NVLink and NVSwitch networking for large-scale AI clusters
